channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israeli doctors say five released Thai hostages in 'fair' health after 15 months of captivity
item.description: BEER YAAKOV, Israel (AP) — When the mother of one of the Thai hostages held in the Gaza Strip for over a year caught sight of her son on a Facebook livestream after his release Thursday, he had changed so much that she didn’t recognize him at first.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israelis and Palestinians rejoice after more hostages and prisoners are freed
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Eight more hostages were freed from the Gaza Strip by Hamas-led militants on Thursday in a sometimes chaotic process that briefly delayed Israel's release of 110 Palestinian prisoners and underscored the fragility of the ceasefire that began earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, WAFAA SHURAFA and ISABEL DeBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Caribbean nation of St Kitts launches investigation after 19 people found dead in drifting vessel
item.description: Authorities in the Caribbean nation of St. Kitts and Nevis have launched an investigation after discovering a partially submerged boat containing at least 19 bodies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's ban on UN's Palestinian aid agency has come into effect. Here's what that looks like
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel on Thursday formally banned the main United Nations aid agency for Palestinian refugees from operating on its territory despite heavy diplomatic backlash, a move that humanitarian officials warn could have a disastrous impact on aid delivery and jeopardize regional stability
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Study finds India doubled its tiger population in a decade and credits conservation efforts
item.description: India doubled its tiger population in a little over a decade by protecting the big cats from poaching and habitat loss, ensuring they have enough prey, reducing human-wildlife conflict, and increasing communities’ living standards near tiger areas.
item.dc:creator: Sibi Arasu, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gisèle Pelicot's ex-husband, imprisoned for raping and drugging her, now caught up in other cases
item.description: NANTERRE, France (AP) — Dominique Pélicot, the convicted rapist who horrified France by drugging his then wife so other men could rape her, was questioned Thursday about other cases of rape and murder that he's suspected in.
item.dc:creator: NICOLAS GARRIGA and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France hands over last military base in Chad
item.description: N’DJAMENA, Chad (AP) — France handed over its last military base in Chad to local authorities on Thursday, two months after the former French colony in central Africa broke off its defense cooperation agreement with Paris.
item.dc:creator: MOUTA ALI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: European Central bank cuts benchmark rate by a quarter percentage point to boost stagnant economy
item.description: FRANKFURT, Germany (AP) — The European Central Bank cut its key interest rate on Thursday to boost an economy that’s struggling to grow as consumers burned by inflation warily eye price tags and businesses try to navigate political turmoil in leading economies France and Germany.
item.dc:creator: DAVID McHUGH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More schools in France are getting special mailboxes for children to flag abuse
item.description: PARIS (AP) — A special mailbox for children to flag abuses was set up Thursday at a Paris primary school, a first in the French capital as part of nationwide effort to encourage more victims of sexual violence to speak up.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Russian drone strikes an apartment building in Ukraine, killing at least 6
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian drone blasted a hole in an apartment building in northeastern Ukraine during a nighttime attack, killing at least six people and wounding nine others, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Hostage releases begin in Gaza Strip
item.description: Hamas handed captive Israeli soldier Agam Berger over to the Red Cross, the first of eight hostages set to be released today.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: European court finds Italy violated the right to life of Italians living in Naples toxic dump area
item.description: ROME (AP) — The European Court of Human Rights ruled Thursday that Italy violated the right to life of Italians living in a toxic-waste polluted area around Naples and ordered the government to develop a strategy to address and monitor the contamination and resulting health problems.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels in eastern Congo say they plan to take their fight to the capital
item.description: Rwanda-backed rebels who have captured eastern Congo’s largest city say they plan to take their rebellion to the capital and seek to gain political power.
item.dc:creator: Chinedu Asadu And Justin Kabumba, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: International Criminal Court has Putin, Netanyahu in its sights, yet its courtrooms are empty
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — For a few hours last week, the International Criminal Court looked poised to take a Libyan warlord into custody. Instead, member state Italy sent the head of a notorious network of detention centers back home.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A New Zealand mountain is granted personhood, recognizing it as sacred for Māori
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — A mountain in New Zealand considered an ancestor by Indigenous people was recognized as a legal person on Thursday after a new law granted it all the rights and responsibilities of a human being.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia comes in from the cold, with flowers blooming around Moscow in January
item.description: January in Russia conjures up images of Muscovites crunching through the snow in bulky coats -- not bunches of delicate snowdrops blooming in grassy areas around still-standing Christmas trees.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA and HARRIET MORRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Woman used wigs, disguises to take British citizenship tests for at least 14 people, authorities say
item.description: A British woman used wigs and disguises to impersonate applicants taking citizenship tests across the United Kingdom, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinians' return to northern Gaza complicates Netanyahu's war aims
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vowed 15 months ago that Israel would achieve “total victory” in the war in Gaza — by eradicating Hamas and freeing all the hostages. One week into a ceasefire with the militant group, many Israelis are dubious.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio exempting 'life-saving humanitarian assistance’ from foreign aid restrictions
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio issued a waiver on Tuesday exempting “life-saving humanitarian assistance” from President Trump’s executive order pausing nearly all foreign aid during a period of review. Trump’s executive order pausing funding, issued on his first day in office, suspended any new U.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Backlash may shatter President Macron's plans to leave mark on Notre Dame windows
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron’s plans for installing contemporary windows inside the restored Notre Dame Cathedral is facing furious opposition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu accepts Trump’s invite, will meet at White House next week
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and President Trump are expected to meet next week in Washington, making him the first foreign leader to visit to the White House since Trump returned to office. The White House confirmed Netanyahu had accepted the invite for the Feb. 4 meeting.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Year of the Snake is underway with festivities around the world
item.description: The holiday — known as the Spring Festival in China, Tet in Vietnam and Seollal in Korea — is a major festival celebrated around the world.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Democrats block GOP's ICC sanctions bill
item.description: Senate Democrats on Tuesday blocked Republicans from advancing legislation to sanction the International Criminal Court (ICC), arguing the bill had sweeping consequences on allies and American businesses.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Former Hamas hostage Naama Levy breaks silence in first message since her release
item.description: Naama Levy, one of the four IDF soldiers released from Hamas captivity on Saturday, writes a message of gratitude in her first statement since arriving home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South African elephant kills tourist who was trying to save children at Kruger National Park
item.description: A tourist at South Africa's Kruger National Park was trampled to death by an elephant as he was trying to protect children, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A weakened Iran has Iraq looking to curb Tehran-backed extremists in country
item.description: The Iraqi government is looking to gain control of Iranian-aligned armed groups that operate inside of Iraq but outside state control, in yet another sign of Iran's weakened position since October 7.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In ‘2000 Meters to Andriivka,’ Oscar winner takes viewers back to Ukraine’s front lines
item.description: Two years after “20 Days in Mariupol” debuted in Park City, Utah, Oscar and Pulitzer Prize winner Mstyslav Chernov is back at the Sundance Film Festival with a new dispatch from Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Lindsey Bahr, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: CDC ordered to stop working with WHO immediately, upending expectations of an extended withdrawal
item.description: President Donald Trump last week issued an executive order to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S. from WHO, but that did not take immediate effect.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: EU military chief says it would make sense to put European troops in Greenland, Welt reports
item.description: Top-ranking European military official Robert Brieger reportedly said it would "make perfect sense" to station troops in Greenland amid President Trump's interest in acquiring it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinians stream back to northern Gaza after Israel lifts closure
item.description: Thousands of Palestinians are returning to the northern Gaza Strip Monday as part of the cease-fire and hostage release deal between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House: Colombia to take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth over immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Holocaust survivor recalls mother's encounter with Nazi
item.description: Marianne Muller joins “NewsNation Prime” to share her family's story of courage and survival during the Holocaust.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: Colombian president orders increase of import tariffs on US goods after Trump order
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro, in a post on the social platform X, said he ordered the "foreign trade minister to raise import tariffs from the U.S. by 25%.
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: Romanian prehistoric gold treasures stolen from Dutch museum
item.description: The Geto-Dacian artifacts belonged to the National History Museum in the capital Bucharest and were on loan to the Drents Museum in the Netherlands.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: CIA believes COVID most likely originated from lab but has low confidence in its own finding
item.description: The CIA now believes the virus responsible for the COVID-19 pandemic most likely originated from a laboratory, according to an assessment released Saturday that points the finger at China even while acknowledging that the spy agency has “low confidence” in its own conclusion.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, WFLA, NBC
